title: Do not create /var/lib/containers/storage/tmp on boot
body:
We seem to be creating this directory on boot, and I find no place where it is used. So remove it and leave it removed.

Based on discussion:
https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20576

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 74303df17292bf642fe8efc99119e65a6130d734. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

The cockpit test failure is completely obvious. Curiously your own test failures look very different (504 Gateway timeout).
```
+ podman save localhost/test-alpine
Error: localhost/test-alpine: image not known
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: open /var/tmp/podman2937744/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/container_images_oci3989760625/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: loading tar component manifest.json: file does not exist
 * dir: open /var/tmp/podman2937744/manifest.json: not a directory
```

https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/3e77f960f65687dcb1d571ebe70278028c6de441
This is used when you set the image copy dir to `storage`
Not sure why this would need to be created in the tmpfile though so I think removing it from there should not cause problems.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Not sure why this would need to be created in the tmpfile though

Agreed -- `podman save` and whatever else uses it should mkdir it on demand.

> so I think removing it from there should not cause problems.

Well, it does as long as that mkdir doesn't happen. So this PR is certainly *not* "LGTM"...
